title: Percentage of Americans who live alone, by age
subtitle: An individual is defined as living alone when there is one person in the household and does not live in group quarters.
caption:
Percentage of Americans who live alone, by age

An individual is defined as living alone when there is one person in the household and does not live in group quarters.

This line chart tracks the percentage of Americans living alone across seven specific age groups from 1900 to 2018. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 45%, while the horizontal axis marks years in 20-year intervals. The data reveals distinct trajectories for different age cohorts. The line representing age 89 begins near 3% in 1900 and rises steadily to become the highest value on the chart by 2018, reaching approximately 42%. The line for age 75 starts as the highest in 1900 at roughly 6%, climbs to a peak of about 32% in 1980, and then declines to roughly 25% by 2018. The age 60 group shows a consistent upward trend, moving from under 5% in 1900 to roughly 18% in 2018. The lines for age 30 and age 45 remain in the lower portion of the chart for the first half of the century before rising; both reach peaks near 10% around the year 2000 before settling slightly lower. The lines for age 21 and age 18 remain at the bottom of the chart throughout the entire period, staying consistently below 6%. The visual arrangement shows that the oldest age groups generally occupy the upper bands of the chart in recent decades, while the youngest groups remain clustered near the bottom axis.
